place: Summit city, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Summit was incorporated as a township in 1869 from parts of New Providence and Springfield, then became a city in 1899.
The town’s name comes from its position atop the Second Watchung Mountain and the early Summit Lodge of jurist James Kent.
The Morris and Essex Railroad, built in 1837, ran over what was then called 'The Summit' hill, giving the town its name.
Summit hosts the Reeves‑Reed Arboretum, a historic country estate turned suburban conservancy that educates visitors about nature.
The city supports public art through Summit Public Art, a volunteer organization founded in 2002 that brings art to public spaces.
